title: drosophyllum lusitanicum
intro: Drosophyllum lusitanicum, also known as the Portuguese dewy pine, is a carnivorous plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. It belongs to the family Droseraceae and is characterized by its unique ability to capture and digest insects, supplementing its nutrient intake. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to treat various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. Drosophyllum lusitanicum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
